item.title: OpenAI Releases Codex Micro, a Stupid $230 Hardware Keypad 摘要
item.description:
OpenAI, after announcing a focus on core product and avoiding distractions, has released a $230 hardware keypad called Codex Micro for use with their Codex tool. This follows their earlier purchase of the TBPN YouTube show for hundreds of millions. Critics, including Quinn Nelson, mock the product as unnecessary since laptops already have built-in keyboards and trackpads. The keypad is seen as another distraction from OpenAI's stated goal of simplifying and refocusing their user experience.
标签: OpenAI, hardware, keypad, Codex, product launch, criticism, distraction, superapp, tech news, absurdity

item.title: Quoting Thibault Sottiaux 摘要
item.description:
Thibault Sottiaux reports that GPT-5.6's Codex agent has been unexpectedly deleting files in certain scenarios. The issue occurs when full access mode is enabled without sandboxing or auto review, the model attempts to override the $HOME environment variable to define a temporary directory, and then mistakenly deletes $HOME instead. This highlights risks of giving AI coding agents unrestricted file system access.
标签: codex, coding-agents, generative-ai, ai, llms, safety, file-deletion, bug, gpt-5.6, sandboxing

item.title: Louie Mantia: ‘The Shape of Apps’ 摘要
item.description:
Louie Mantia discusses the squircle mandate for Mac app icons, arguing that while it improves mediocre icons, it also restricts great ones, pushing them toward mediocrity. He prefers Apple allowing designers to poke outside the squircle boundary, noting that some favorite icons did that, but least-favorite ones ignored the shape entirely. Despite the loss, he acknowledges multiple ways to design within the squircle. The piece defends Apple's direction in unifying app icon design.
标签: app icons, squircle, Apple, design, Mac, iOS, UI, icon design, controversy, platform design

item.title: Pluralistic: Deranged billionaires and their syndromes (16 Jul 2026) 摘要
item.description:
The article critiques how billionaires and neoclassical economists rationalize monopoly power through theories like 'consumer welfare,' 'revealed preferences,' and 'meritocracy.' It argues that successful capitalists often become rentiers who use captured institutions to defend their wealth, contradicting market ideals. The author links leftist and pro-market critiques of monopoly, emphasizing that without competitive pressure, profits become rents. The piece also touches on regulatory capture, the fallacy of revealed preferences under coercion, and the Puritan roots of meritocracy as a justification for inequality.
标签: billionaires, monopoly, neoclassical economics, regulatory capture, meritocracy, antitrust, market theory, political economy, inequality, capitalism

item.title: Lawyer for Apple Mixed Up Two OpenAI Employees’ Names, Sent One Email to the Wrong Guy, Back in February 摘要
item.description:
Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI alleges trade secret theft and claims OpenAI never responded to concerns raised in February. However, NBC News reviewed emails showing OpenAI did respond, but communications stalled after Apple's outside counsel, Gabriel Gross, mistakenly emailed an OpenAI employee named Chang instead of Wang, confusing the two. Gross apologized, but OpenAI's general counsel asked Apple to remove him from the case, which Apple declined. OpenAI claims the issue was resolved after Gross's apology, but the author argues this does not refute Apple's claim of no substantive response to their initial letter.
标签: Apple, OpenAI, lawsuit, trade secrets, legal, email mix-up, NBC News, corporate dispute

item.title: OpenAI Takes a Second Crack at a Response to Apple’s Trade Secret Theft Lawsuit 摘要
item.description:
OpenAI responded to a trade secret theft lawsuit from Apple, stating they are unaware of any evidence supporting the complaint's merit. The phrasing 'not aware of any evidence' is noted as a curious and weaker defense than outright denial. This follows a previous similar response, suggesting a pattern in OpenAI's legal strategy.
标签: OpenAI, Apple, lawsuit, trade secrets, legal, response, Bloomberg, tech

item.title: Kimi K3, and what we can still learn from the pelican benchmark 摘要
item.description:
Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion parameter model (rounded to 3T), claiming it as the first open 3T-class model. It outperforms Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 on self-reported benchmarks but trails Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol. Pricing is $3/M input and $15/M output tokens, making it the most expensive Chinese AI model. The model excels in frontend code generation and long-horizon knowledge tasks. Simon Willison tested it via OpenRouter, generating an SVG of a pelican riding a bicycle, costing $0.25. He notes the model's heavy reasoning token usage and hidden system prompt, while praising its vision capabilities.
标签: AI, LLM, Moonshot AI, Kimi K3, model release, benchmarks, pricing, open weights, reasoning, vision

item.title: Dithering: ‘Apple Sues OpenAI’ 摘要
item.description:
John Gruber promotes a free episode of the Dithering podcast, where he and Ben Thompson discuss Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI. Gruber offers a unique perspective on the case, differing from mainstream commentary. The episode is available for free on the web, though not via RSS. He encourages subscriptions to Dithering ($7/month or $70/year, or included with Stratechery Plus), which releases two 15-minute episodes weekly.
标签: Apple, OpenAI, lawsuit, podcast, Dithering, John Gruber, Ben Thompson, Stratechery, tech news, AI

item.title: Firefox in WebAssembly 摘要
item.description:
Puter compiled Firefox to WebAssembly, enabling the entire browser to run inside another browser. The project used Firefox/Gecko for its strong single-process support and cost an estimated $25,000 in Claude Opus and Fable tokens via a Claude Max subscription. Traffic is proxied through Puter's server using the Wisp protocol over WebSocket, supporting end-to-end encryption. The demo loads a 233MB gecko.wasm and 18MB chrome-assets.tar.zst. The source code is available on GitHub, and a similar project compiles WebKit to WASM.
标签: Firefox, WebAssembly, browser, WASM, Puter, Claude, AI-assisted programming, WebSocket, Wisp protocol, end-to-end encryption
